heading: Release or detention of a defendant pending sentence or appeal
label: 3143 Release or detention of a defendant pending sentence or appeal

children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the judicial officer shall order that a person who has been found guilty of an offense and who is awaiting imposition or execution of sentence, other than a person for whom the applicable guideline promulgated pursuant to does not recommend a term of imprisonment, be detained, unless the judicial officer finds by clear and convincing evidence that the person is not likely to flee or pose a danger to the safety of any other person or the community if released under section 3142(b) or (c). If the judicial officer makes such a finding, such judicial officer shall order the release of the person in accordance with section 3142(b) or (c).28 U.S.C. 994

children.label: i
children.text: the judicial officer finds there is a substantial likelihood that a motion for acquittal or new trial will be granted; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an attorney for the Government has recommended that no sentence of imprisonment be imposed on the person; and

children.label: B
children.text: the judicial officer finds by clear and convincing evidence that the person is not likely to flee or pose a danger to any other person or the community.

children.heading: .—Release or Detention Pending Appeal by the Defendant
children.label: b .—Release or Detention Pending Appeal by the Defendant
A
children.text: by clear and convincing evidence that the person is not likely to flee or pose a danger to the safety of any other person or the community if released under section 3142(b) or (c) of this title; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an order for a new trial,

children.label: iii
children.text: a sentence that does not include a term of imprisonment, or

children.label: iv
children.text: a reduced sentence to a term of imprisonment less than the total of the time already served plus the expected duration of the appeal process.

The judicial officer shall order that a person who has been found guilty of an offense in a case described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of subsection (f)(1) of section 3142 and sentenced to a term of imprisonment, and who has filed an appeal or a petition for a writ of certiorari, be detained.

children.heading: .—Release or Detention Pending Appeal by the Government
children.label: c .—Release or Detention Pending Appeal by the Government

children.text: if the person has been sentenced to a term of imprisonment, order that person detained; and
in any other circumstance, release or detain the person under section 3142.
